_meta.title: @agent-ui/router v1 — memory-first SPA router with opt-in URL reflection (build decomposition)
_meta.author: system-planner
_meta.grounding: Kim's verbatim ask: a client-side SPA router, 'primarily memory-based, but with URL state reflection' (greenfield — grep-verified 2026-07-08: no router exists anywhere in the repo; the only mention is agent-app-surfaces.prd.md's non-goal 'the app brings its own router/store'). Upstream: reactive/graph.ts (signal/computed/effect/createScope — route state is a signal, navigation a function) · dom/element.ts (UIElement connect-scope/abort lifetimes; traits are (host,opts)=>cleanup invoked from connected()) · dom/index.ts (html``/render are PRIVATE — ADR-0023 keeps directives/mount the only public render units, so the outlet seam must be element-factory-based, not TemplateResult-based) · ADR-0114 (ui-text as='a' is fixed target=_blank + rel policy — unusable for in-app nav until its foreseen `target` extension lands) · ADR-0082..0084 + m1-app-shell.decomp.json (the new-package pattern: skeleton, layering trip-wire, descriptor, size line-item, reviewer gate) · ADR-0040 (size gate is manual) · CLAUDE.md layering law (shared ← components ← {a2ui, router} ← app; nothing imports upward).
_meta.note: Both planes derived independently: OUTSIDE-IN from the package's structural grain (infra → headless core → URL adapter → elements → gates → teaching → barrel), INSIDE-OUT from the ask's verb set (match, hold-as-signal, navigate, traverse, reflect, adopt, guard-the-single-source, render, lazy-load, tear down, trigger-from-markup, preserve-native-anchor, announce-active) plus the standing fleet doctrine (layering, budget, cross-engine truth, descriptors, independent review). Design records: ADR-0115 (proposed, NOT self-ratified — Kim offline) · prd/router.prd.md · spec/router.spec.md · lld/router.lld.md. Coordinator-reserved number 0115 (README tail = 0114; 0108 is a known hole, never reused).

nodes.label: @agent-ui/router v1
package infrastructure
package skeleton (package.json exports map, tsconfig extending the strict base, workspace row)
nodes.accept: `npm run check` exits 0 with @agent-ui/router resolving; dependencies exactly {@agent-ui/components, @agent-ui/shared}; no other package.json changed

nodes.label: layering trip-wire (router/src/layering.test.ts + apex-un-imported grep)
nodes.accept: `npm test` includes router layering.test.ts green: every import under router/src resolves to {@agent-ui/components, @agent-ui/shared} or a local path; repo grep proves no source under components/src, a2ui/src, shared/src imports @agent-ui/router; a planted upward import on a unique token goes RED (negative control, grep-confirmed applied, then reverted)

nodes.label: headless core (src/core/ — zero DOM globals)
route matcher (core/matcher.ts — static/:param/trailing-* grammar, first-match-wins, trailing-slash normalization, query parse)
nodes.accept: jsdom-free unit suite green: static and :param extraction, trailing * capture, /a/ ≡ /a, ?k=v parsed onto the match, declaration-order precedence, no-match → null; SPEC-R3 AC table covered 1:1

nodes.label: memory history (core/history.ts — entry stack + index, push/replace/go)
nodes.accept: unit suite green: push truncates the forward tail, replace swaps in place, back/forward move the index and clamp at the ends without throwing; the stack is inspectable for the URL adapter's stamped-state sync

nodes.label: router instance (core/router.ts — createRouter: route signal, navigate/back/forward/dispose; the headless invariant)
nodes.accept: unit suite green: route is a ReadonlySignal, navigate/back/forward mutate it through the memory stack, initial route seeds from options; the no-DOM gate passes — a static scan asserts core/*.ts references none of window/document/location/history (and it goes RED on a planted reference — negative control)

nodes.label: URL reflection adapter (src/url.ts — opt-in, one function, two strategies)
hash strategy (default: #/path?query ↔ route; hashchange inbound)
nodes.accept: jsdom suite green: connectUrl(router) with default mode writes location.hash on navigate, adopts an existing #/deep-link at connect (deep-link-wins, replace semantics), and routes an external hashchange as a push navigation

nodes.label: history strategy (opt-in: pushState/replaceState + popstate; state-stamped index sync)
nodes.accept: jsdom suite green: navigate → pushState with {uiRouter:{index}} stamped; popstate with a stamped state re-points the memory index (back/forward stay aligned with the browser stack); un-stamped popstate falls back to path adoption

nodes.label: adoption + echo-loop lock + teardown (deep-link-wins rule, directional write lock, cleanup restores memory-only operation)
nodes.accept: jsdom suite green: a reflected write's own popstate/hashchange echo does not re-navigate (directional lock — the coerceAttribute precedent); after cleanup() no URL write occurs on navigate and no listener remains (AbortController-backed); double-connect throws or no-ops documented

nodes.label: elements (controls/ — the rendering + trigger seams)
ui-router-outlet (controls/router-outlet/ — .router prop, factory→element swap, async last-wins, zero-residue teardown) + descriptor
nodes.accept: jsdom suite green: assigning .router renders the matched route's factory element, a route change swaps the child and the previous element is disconnected (its effects/listeners provably dead — the UIElement zero-residue contract), a stale async factory resolution is discarded (last-navigation-wins), null route renders nothing; router-outlet.md descriptor passes the contract↔props trip-wire

nodes.label: ui-router-link (controls/router-link/ — to/replace props, real <a> stamp, plain-activation intercept, modified-click fallthrough, aria-current) + descriptor
nodes.accept: jsdom suite green: the stamp is a real <a> with href derived from the attached strategy (hash form when memory-only — documented degradation), plain click preventDefaults and navigates, ctrl/cmd/shift/middle clicks do NOT preventDefault, aria-current='page' present exactly on the active link and absent otherwise; router-link.md descriptor passes the trip-wire

nodes.label: gates & proof
cross-engine browser legs (Chromium + WebKit)
nodes.accept: test:browser green in BOTH engines: a real click on ui-router-link swaps outlet content with NO document navigation (the SPA guarantee), a modified click is left to the platform, aria-current styling + forced-colors legibility hold, whole-shape non-zero boxes for swapped content; each leg has a biting negative control

nodes.label: size line-item + tree-shake proof (scripts/measure-size.mjs gains ONE @agent-ui/router row)
nodes.accept: manual `npm run size` (ADR-0040 posture) reports the router line-item within the provisional ≤4.0 KB gz marginal budget; a tree-shake probe proves importing the core drags neither element; a planted over-budget number fails the gate (negative control)

nodes.label: independent component-reviewer pass (generator ≠ critic, before the v1 commit)
nodes.accept: component-reviewer records GO with both axes ≥4 and zero blockers on ui-router-outlet + ui-router-link BEFORE the commit; a NO-GO loops back to the owning slice

nodes.label: teaching / dogfood
router doc/demo page (site/router-doc.html + demo wiring; nav row; the site stays MPA)
nodes.accept: `npm run check` (incl. check:site) + `npm run build` green with the new page; the page demos memory-mode navigation live (outlet + links + back/forward) and states the URL-reflection opt-in; site nav/toc gates stay green

nodes.label: public barrel + subpath exports (src/index.ts + exports map finalization — serial integration slice)
nodes.accept: index.ts exports createRouter/connectUrl/types + the element classes; package.json exposes './router-outlet' and './router-link' subpaths (the icons/app pure-core+subpath pattern) so core consumers never pay for elements; `npm run check && npm test` green

actions.label: match a path against the route table (static, :param, trailing *)
yield null on no-match (consumer 404 via a * route)
carry query params on the match
hold the current route as reactive state (signal read/subscribe)
navigate programmatically (push / replace)
traverse back/forward with no URL involved
seed the initial route
operate with zero DOM (non-browser host; headless tests)
reflect route → URL (hash default; history opt-in)
adopt URL → route (deep link at connect; popstate/hashchange inbound)
keep one source of truth (echo-loop directional lock)
detach reflection cleanly (cleanup → memory-only operation restored)
render the matched content (factory → element swap)
lazy-load a route's content (async factory, last-navigation-wins)
tear down replaced content with zero residue
trigger navigation from markup (click/Enter on a link)
preserve native anchor affordances (href, modified/middle-click fallthrough)
announce the active link (aria-current='page')
enforce the layering law (imports inward only; apex un-imported)
hold the size budget and tree-shake cleanly
prove interactive behavior cross-engine (Chromium + WebKit)
teach the API (descriptors + a live doc/demo page)
pass independent review before commit (generator ≠ critic)
export the public surface (barrel + element subpaths)
stand the package in the workspace DAG (resolvable under the strict TS posture, correct dependency set)

edges.why: the trip-wire scans a package that must exist and resolve first
core files live in the package the skeleton creates
core files live in the package the skeleton creates
the router composes the matcher (route resolution on navigate)
the router composes the memory stack (back/forward semantics)
the hash strategy drives the router's navigate/route contract
the history strategy drives the same contract and reads the stack index to stamp state
the lock/adoption/teardown layer wraps both strategies — hash must exist to be wrapped
the lock/adoption/teardown layer wraps both strategies — history must exist to be wrapped
the outlet consumes the router instance contract (.router prop + route signal)
the link consumes the router contract (navigate + active-route signal + strategy-derived href)
browser legs click links and observe outlet swaps — both elements must exist
browser legs click links and observe outlet swaps — both elements must exist
the barrel exports the finished adapter surface (serial integration, single writer)
the barrel + subpaths export the finished outlet
the barrel + subpaths export the finished link
size + tree-shake measure the final public surface, not a moving target
the doc page demos the shipped elements
the doc page demos the shipped elements
the reviewer scores browser-proven behavior, not intentions
the reviewer's GO covers the budget posture (the whole-shape + size lesson)
